message.content: Adversaries may exploit public WHOIS databases to gather information about potential victims, leveraging the data stored by regional Internet registries (RIRs). WHOIS queries can reveal details such as domain names, IP blocks, contact information, and DNS nameservers. This information can be used for further reconnaissance, such as active scanning or phishing, and to establish or compromise infrastructure. Common exploitation methods include using online resources or command-line utilities to access WHOIS data. The potential impact includes increased risk of targeted attacks, such as phishing or unauthorized access to external remote services. Mitigation strategies involve limiting the exposure of sensitive information in WHOIS records, using privacy protection services offered by domain registrars, and monitoring for unusual WHOIS queries related to your domains.
